name: Itoiz Reservoir Loop from Aoiz
description: This difficult 45.1-mile touring cycling route near Aoiz, Erro, and Aurizberri offers varied landscapes and cultural highlights.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Aoiz/Agoitz traverse a landscape characterized by river valleys, reservoirs, and undulating terrain. The region features a mix of natural and historical elements, with routes often incorporating scenic waterways and notable bridges. Many of the best touring bike trails in Aoiz/Agoitz are loop routes, providing varied scenery and return to the starting point. Best touring cycling routes around Aoiz/Agoitz The most popular touring cycling route is Itoiz Reservoir Loop from Aoiz, a 45.1 miles (72.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 55 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive views around the Itoiz Reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Portal de Francia – Magdalena Bridge loop from Lizoain, a difficult 35.6 miles (57.3 km) path. This route features historical bridges and varied terrain through the countryside. Local touring cyclists also love the Urrobi Valley – Urrobi Reservoir loop from Nagore, a 25.7 miles (41.4 km) trail leading through the Urrobi Valley and past the Urrobi Reservoir, often completed in about 2 hours 44 minutes. Touring cycling around Aoiz/Agoitz is defined by river valleys, reservoirs, and historical bridges, offering a diverse cycling experience. The network includes options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Aoiz/Agoitz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from 4 reviews. More than 80 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Aoiz/Agoitz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Aoiz/Agoitz?
A: There are over 10 touring cycling routes in the Aoiz/Agoitz region, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists. These routes traverse river valleys, reservoirs, and undulating terrain, often incorporating scenic waterways and historical bridges.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes for beginners in Aoiz/Agoitz?
A: Yes, Aoiz/Agoitz offers a couple of easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes typically feature less elevation gain and shorter distances, allowing you to enjoy the scenery at a comfortable pace.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Aoiz/Agoitz?
A: The touring cycling routes around Aoiz/Agoitz are characterized by a mix of river valleys, reservoirs, and undulating terrain. You'll encounter both natural landscapes and historical elements, with many routes featuring scenic waterways and notable bridges. Expect some climbs and descents as you navigate the region's varied topography.

Q: Are there any loop touring cycling routes in the Aoiz/Agoitz area?
A: Yes, many of the best touring cycling trails in Aoiz/Agoitz are loop routes. This design allows for varied scenery throughout your ride and conveniently brings you back to your starting point. An example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974125875/itoiz-reservoir-loop-from-aoiz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Itoiz Reservoir Loop from Aoiz&lt;/a&gt;, which offers extensive views.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions along the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes often incorporate significant natural and historical elements. You can expect to see scenic waterways, reservoirs like Itoiz and Urrobi, and historical bridges. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31866989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portal de Francia – Magdalena Bridge loop from Lizoain&lt;/a&gt; features several historical bridges and varied countryside.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Aoiz/Agoitz?
A: The touring cycling routes in Aoiz/Agoitz are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, including river valleys and reservoirs, and the presence of historical bridges that add character to the rides.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes in Aoiz/Agoitz?
A: Yes, for those seeking a longer challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974125875/itoiz-reservoir-loop-from-aoiz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Itoiz Reservoir Loop from Aoiz&lt;/a&gt; cover approximately 45 miles (72.6 km) and take nearly 5 hours to complete, offering extensive views and a significant workout.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through the Urrobi Valley?
A: Absolutely. The Urrobi Valley is a prominent feature in the region's cycling landscape. You can explore it on routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24644356" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urrobi Valley – Urrobi Reservoir loop from Nagore&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through the valley and past the reservoir, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31968943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urrobi Valley – Erro Village Church loop from Lizoain&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there routes that start directly from Aoiz/Agoitz?
A: Yes, several routes are designed to start directly from Aoiz/Agoitz, making them convenient for local cyclists or visitors staying in the town. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15007934" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval Bridge of Aoiz loop from Aoiz / Agoitz&lt;/a&gt;, which is a challenging 26 km ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Aoiz/Agoitz?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, regions with river valleys and reservoirs like Aoiz/Agoitz typically offer pleasant cycling conditions in spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially in the mornings or late afternoons, though some routes may have less shade.

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, in addition to easy and difficult options, Aoiz/Agoitz also features a couple of moderate touring cycling routes. These provide a balanced challenge, suitable for cyclists with some experience looking for a step up from beginner trails without committing to the most demanding climbs.
tips: Aoiz is only 28km from Pamplona. Among the most important monuments of Aoiz we find the Church of San Miguel from the 14th century, the monastery of Aoiz from the 11th century, and the Medieval Bridge of Aoiz.
A hydraulic work located in the vicinity of the Villa de Aoiz. It takes its name from one of the towns it floods.
A small town founded in 1269 by Teobaldo II. There are many dolmens to see in the area (about 10), of which four are in good condition (Dondoro, Artzilo, Mendiaundi and Baratzeko Erreka).
The landscape of Erro is beautiful. At the top of the port it is convenient to stop to contemplate the panorama. An ideal place for nature lovers.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Aoiz, Erro, Aurizberri – Circular Route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is categorized as a difficult bike tour. It spans 72.6 kilometers with a significant elevation gain of 1,060 meters, requiring very good fitness. However, it does not necessitate special cycling skills.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features paved surfaces. As you cycle, you'll experience a varied landscape, particularly around Aurizberri/Espinal, which is situated in the picturesque Merindad de Sangüesa in Navarra.

faqs.question: What are some notable points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several interesting spots, including the Medieval Bridge of Aoiz and the scenic Itoiz Reservoir. Near Aurizberri/Espinal, you can find well-preserved dolmens (Dondoro, Artzilo, Mendiaundi, Baratzeko Erreka) and a local cheese factory. The Erro Village Church is also a highlight.

faqs.question: Is the Aoiz, Erro, Aurizberri – Circular Route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is not recommended for families or beginners. It is a difficult bike tour requiring very good fitness due to its length (72.6 km) and significant elevation gain (1,060 meters).

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: While specific seasonal recommendations aren't provided, touring cycling routes in Navarra are generally best enjoyed in spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the scenery is vibrant. Summer can be hot, and winter may bring challenging weather conditions.

faqs.question: Where can I park and what is the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one, starting and ending in Aoiz. You can typically find parking within the town of Aoiz to begin your tour. Specific trailhead parking details are not provided, but public parking areas in Aoiz would be your starting point.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Aoiz, Erro, Aurizberri – Circular Route?
faqs.answer: As this is a touring bicycle route primarily on paved surfaces and public roads, dogs are generally permitted if they can accompany you safely, for example, in a bike trailer. Always ensure your dog is leashed and under control, especially when passing through villages or near livestock.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Aoiz, Erro, Aurizberri – Circular Route. It utilizes public roads and paths.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Roman road of the Pyrenees, Camino Francés - 01, and the European long distance path E3 - part Spain.

faqs.question: Are there opportunities to purchase local products along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, near Aurizberri/Espinal, there is a cheese factory located near the church where you can observe cheese-making and purchase local products, offering a cultural and culinary stop during your ride.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a challenging touring bicycle route like this?
faqs.answer: Given its length and difficulty, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bicycle, appropriate cycling attire, sun protection, and a first-aid kit. A fully charged phone and navigation device are also highly recommended.
